title: Implement isValidEmail() in validators.ts
prompt:
src/validators.ts is missing an `isValidEmail` function. Implement and export it from src/validators.ts so the tests in tests/target.test.ts pass.

Spec (derived from the tests):
- Signature: `isValidEmail(value: unknown): boolean`.
- Returns false for any non-string input (null, undefined, numbers, objects).
- Trims surrounding whitespace before validating; whitespace-only and empty strings are invalid.
- Validation is case-insensitive.
- The address must contain exactly one `@`.
- The local part (before `@`) must be non-empty.
- The domain (after `@`) must be non-empty AND must contain at least one `.` (so plain hostnames like `localhost` are rejected).
- Common patterns like dots and `+tag` in the local part, and multi-label domains like `example.co.uk`, must be accepted.

Do NOT modify the existing `isNonEmptyString` or `normalizeEmail` functions — they are covered by tests/regression.test.ts and must keep passing. Do not modify any test files. Keep the change confined to src/validators.ts.
